Title: 1,453 migrants detained, Interpol fugitive and 'flying passport' ring exposed in Johor blitz
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A South Korean man wanted under an Interpol Red Notice and a "flying passport" syndicate involving Vietnamese travel documents are among six major cases uncovered in Johor's immigration enforcement blitz.
The cases are part of a string of cross-border offences detected during 458 enforcement operations conducted between January and April, which led to the detention of 1,453 migrants for various immigration violations across the state.
State Immigration director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said that on April 8, enforcement officers raided a luxury residence in Horizon Hills, Iskandar Puteri, and detained a South Korean man listed under an Interpol Red Notice for alleged fraud involving a face mask business scandal in his home country.
An Interpol Red Notice is an international alert issued to locate and provisionally arrest a wanted person pending extradition or legal proceedings.
Rusdi said the man's passport had been revoked by the Republic of Korea, and investigations showed he had overstayed in Malaysia following the cancellation of his travel document.
He was also found to have harboured a Filipino woman who had overstayed her permitted period in the country.
The South Korean man was compounded RM6,000 for multiple offences, with the penalty issued following diplomatic requests from the Korean Embassy to expedite his removal for further action in his home country.
The Filipino woman was sentenced to four months' jail after pleading guilty to overstaying in Malaysia.
In another case on Jan 29, immigration officers dismantled a suspected "flying passport" syndicate operating from a unit at Residensi ARC in Taman Daya, Johor Baru.
A Malaysian man was detained after nine Vietnamese passports were found in his possession, believed to have been unlawfully held for endorsement and movement under an irregular arrangement.
Rusdi said the passports were kept without lawful authority and were suspected to be part of a wider illegal facilitation network involving foreign travel documents.
The suspect was charged under Section 12(1)(f) of the Passports Act 1966 for unlawful possession of other people's travel documents and fined RM9,000 to RM1,000 for each passport seized.
On March 31, a raid at Crescent Bay Suites here saw officers detain a Mexican man, two Chinese men and a Hong Kong woman.
Thirteen passports of various nationalities were found in the Mexican man's possession.
Rusdi said the suspect was believed to have tampered with travel documents in an attempt to remain in Malaysia illegally.
He was charged under the Immigration Act for falsifying documents and unlawful possession of foreign passports.
The Hong Kong woman was found to have overstayed and was also in possession of another person's travel document, while the two Chinese men were found without valid identification.
The Mexican man claimed trial and was remanded at Penjara Reman Ulu Choh pending further proceedings.
On April 29, immigration authorities dismantled a foreign-run online gambling syndicate operating from Puteri Harbour, resulting in the arrest of two Chinese men, a boy and five Thai women.
Authorities also seized RM2,500 in cash, 34 mobile phones, seven computers and a printer believed to have been used in cross-border gambling activities targeting users in Thailand.
In an earlier operation on Jan 6, three Indonesian women believed to have been exploited were rescued from a factory in Taman Mount Austin.
Three Malaysian men were detained, with investigations carried out under the Anti-Trafficking in Persons and Smuggling of Migrants Act 2007.
Rusdi said the series of operations reflected ongoing efforts to dismantle transnational crime networks operating in the state.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Only 0.1pct of Kedah padi fields planted amid heatwave
Article:
ALOR STAR: Only 0.1 per cent of padi fields under the Muda Agricultural Development Authority (Mada) have begun planting due to the prolonged heatwave and low rainfall.
Mada chairman Datuk Dr Ismail Salleh said that although the planting rate is low, more than half of the areas are ready for cultivation.
He said monitoring up to April 29 showed that 52.9 per cent of the areas had completed land preparation.
"So far, only 0.1 per cent of the areas have started planting, but this does not mean activities have not fully begun.
"Farmers are now in the preparation phase and waiting for sufficient rain before planting is carried out on a wider scale," he told a press conference at the Mada headquarters here today.
Ismail said the delay is influenced by weather conditions, with recorded rainfall far lower than usual.
"The average rainfall up to May 3 was only 233 millimetres, which is 43 per cent lower than the 20-year average of 411 millimetres. The hot and dry conditions with daily temperatures exceeding 35 degrees Celsius also contributed to this delay," he said.
Meanwhile, he said water levels at the Pedu, Muda, and Ahning dams have also dropped significantly.
He added that total water storage is now at 38.41 per cent, which is 67.91 per cent lower than in the same period last year.
Ismail said the situation directly affects the ability to release water to support padi planting activities.
He said his agency does not deny that the delay could affect padi production if it continues.
"The risks include disruptions to mechanisation operations, water management, and potential yield damage if the flowering and harvesting seasons occur during the rain. It could also disrupt the entire annual planting schedule," he said.
However, he said various measures are being taken, including close monitoring, rain-seeking prayers, and cloud seeding operations to increase rainfall.
He said the planting schedule remains under control, with the deadline for seeding for Phases 1, 2, and 3 set for May 15, May 29, and June 12, respectively.
Therefore, he advised farmers to be ready to start planting as soon as there is sufficient rain.
"The coordination of field activities is crucial to ensure the country's padi production chain is not affected," he said.
Yesterday, the media reported that nearly 90 per cent of the 56,000 farmers in Kedah, including those in the Mada area, have not started planting for the 1/2026 season due to rising operating costs and a lack of water supply.
Malaysian Padi Farmers Association (Pesawah) chairman Abdul Rashid Yob said the situation not only disrupts the planting schedule but could also have a long-term impact on the country's rice self-sufficiency level.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ministry explores tube well development to safeguard against future water shortages
Article:
LIPIS: The Energy Transition and Water Transformation Ministry is conducting a study on the development of tube wells as a preparatory measure to address potential water crisis in the future.
Deputy Minister Datuk Seri Abdul Rahman Mohamad said the study, carried out by the National Water Research Institute of Malaysia (NAHRIM), aims to identify new water sources as a support plan in the event of supply disruptions.
"We are currently facing uncertain weather conditions such as drought and low rainfall. Based on forecasts, the hot weather is expected to continue until August, which could affect water resources.
"Nevertheless, through the efforts of all relevant agencies, water supply nationwide remains under control. However, consumers must play an important role in reducing wastage," he told reporters after officiating the Selamat Air Raya programme at Dewan Jubli Perak Sultan Ahmad Shah here.
On March 21, it was reported that the United Nations University Institute for Water, Environment and Health (UNU-INWEH) had warned that the world is entering an era of "global water bankruptcy".
Rahman said water consumption in Peninsular Malaysia and Labuan is higher at around 223 litres per capita per day compared with the 165 litres per capita per day recommended by the World Health Organisation (WHO).
"This high level of water usage must be reduced. If the public does not play its part, we risk facing a water crisis. All parties must take responsibility to ensure this can be avoided," he said.
He added that the National Water Services Commission (Span) is also implementing the Communication, Education and Public Awareness Programme, targeting to reach at least one million users nationwide throughout 2026.
"This initiative aims to raise public awareness on efficient water usage and the importance of avoiding wastage to ensure the sustainability of the nation's water supply," he said.
At the programme, Span distributed zakat wakalah totalling RM104,000 to 200 students from primary, secondary schools and higher education institutions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: Abu Dhabi's Lulu Group to buy RM400mil of Malaysian halal products
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Abu Dhabi-based retail giant Lulu Group plans to procure halal products worth US$100 million (over RM400 million) from Malaysia, says Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
Zahid said the commitment was conveyed during a recent meeting between Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Lulu Group owner Yusuffali M.A.
He said the initiative would open a significant pathway for Malaysian halal products – particularly those produced by Bumiputera entrepreneurs – to penetrate international markets.
He said that he had proposed, in Anwar's presence, for Lulu Group to work directly with Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) in sourcing halal products for global distribution.
"I told him not to look elsewhere, but to work with Mara to procure these halal products for the world market.
"I will ask the Mara chairman and director-general to coordinate with his appointed representative in Malaysia," he said in his speech at the launch of Mara's halal ecosystem at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur today.
Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister, said the collaboration would allow Malaysian halal products to be marketed through Lulu's supermarket network worldwide, particularly in the Middle East.
He said the group aims to commence the arrangement as early as July 1.
He said that the halal industry should no longer be viewed merely as a certification label, but as a comprehensive value chain anchored in trust, quality and integrity.
"Halal is not only for Muslims. It is for all religions, all communities and the global market," he added.
Zahid said that the global halal industry was projected to reach US$5 trillion by 2030, adding that Malaysia must go beyond its strong certification framework to capitalise on the sector's growth.
He said Mara had a pivotal role in ensuring Bumiputera entrepreneurs were active participants in the expanding global halal economy.
"I do not want Bumiputera entrepreneurs to remain mere observers. They must emerge as global players and gain international recognition," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran foreign ministry says US must 'abandon its excessive demands'
Article:
TEHERAN: Iran's foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said today that the United States must reduce its demands on the Islamic republic, with negotiations stalled over ending the two-month-long Middle East war.
"At this stage, our priority is to end the war," he said in a briefing broadcast by state television. "The other side must commit to a reasonable approach and abandon its excessive demands regarding Iran."
Negotiations between the two countries have been stalled since a ceasefire took effect on April 8, with only one round of direct peace talks held so far.
A key sticking point has been Iran's stranglehold on the Strait of Hormuz since the US and Israel launched strikes on Feb 28, choking off major flows of oil, gas and fertiliser, while the United States has imposed a blockade on Iranian ports.
But US President Donald Trump said on Sunday that Washington would begin escorting ships through the waterway, drawing a threat of attacks from the Iranian military.
"By now, the Americans should have learned that they cannot use the language of threats and force against the Iranian nation," said Baqaei.
"The Islamic Republic of Iran has shown that it considers itself the guardian and protector of the Strait of Hormuz and this vital waterway."
He said the strait was a "secure and safe route" for international shipping before the war.
"The international community must hold the United States and the Zionist regime accountable for imposing insecurity on this waterway and for creating problems that are being felt across the world," he added, referring to Israel.
Baqaei told state television before Trump's announcement of an escort plan that Teheran had submitted a 14-point proposal "focused on ending the war," and that Washington had responded to it in a message to Pakistani mediators. - REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rosmaini to face fresh charges after getting maximum 14-year sentence
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Trader Rosmaini Abdul Raof, whose prison sentence was increased to the maximum of 14 years by the Court of Appeal for an assault that left his pregnant ex-wife in a coma, is set to face new charges.
The new charges are linked to a separate alleged domestic abuse case involving his current wife, who was pregnant and had suffered a miscarriage.
Lawyer Kamal Hisham Ja'afar said Rosmaini, 43, is expected to face the charges at the Alor Star courts.
"Rosmaini is currently being held at Kajang Prison, where he will remain at least until Wednesday before being brought back to Alor Star to face charges for allegedly causing injuries to his wife," he said.
Rosmaini had earlier been brought to the appellate court today from Alor Star, Kedah, while under a five-day remand for the latest domestic abuse case.
The hearing was for his appeal against his conviction and 10-year jail sentence imposed by the Johor Baru Sessions Court in 2024 and upheld by the High Court last year.
Following the decision, Kamal Hisham said Rosmaini maintained his innocence of assaulting his ex-wife, Jahidah Nordin @ Sylvester, 48, in an attack five years ago that left her in a coma.
She remains in a vegetative state.
Jahidah's son, now four years old, whom she gave birth to via caesarean section while still in a coma on Nov 30, 2021, has developmental issues and has been certified as disabled.
Kamal Hisham said Rosmaini accepted the appellate court's ruling with an open heart.
"However, he firmly denies ever assaulting Jahidah.
"He will not be appealing to the Federal Court," he said.
Kamal Hisham said he had represented Rosmaini pro bono in the case involving Jahidah and would no longer act for him in future proceedings.
A three-judge panel led by Datuk Azman Abdullah, who sat with Datuk Noorin Badaruddin and Datuk Mohd Radzi Abdul Hamid, unanimously increased the sentence from the original 10-year term imposed by the Sessions Court and upheld by the High Court, describing it as insufficient for a violent domestic case where a husband attacked his vulnerable, pregnant wife.
The panel also ordered Rosmaini to pay RM25,000 in compensation to Jahidah through her sister, Fazariah, or face an additional six months in jail if he fails to do so.
In 2021, Jahidah, who was a civil servant, was treated at the Sultanah Aminah Hospital's Intensive Care Unit in Johor Bahru before she was transferred to Tuanku Ja'afar Hospital in Seremban, Negri Sembilan.
In 2023, the Seremban Syariah Lower Court approved a fasakh (divorce) application from Jahidah.
Today marks the
fifth year
since the incident occurred.
Rosmaini was charged with deliberately causing grievous hurt to Jahidah at 6am on April 4, 2021 at an apartment unit in Larkin, Johor Bahru.
He was charged under Section 325 of the Penal Code read together with Section 326A of the same Code.
The charge under Section 325 carries an imprisonment term of up to seven years and a fine upon conviction, while Section 326A, which deals with punishment for committing domestic violence, provides for a sentence of up to double the maximum term of imprisonment.
On June 17, 2021, Rosmaini pleaded not guilty to the charge.
He subsequently filed a notice of appeal in November last year after High Court Judge Atan Mustaffa Yussof Ahmad dismissed his appeal against both conviction and sentence, which had been imposed by Johor Baru Sessions Court judge Fatimah Zahari on Dec 9, 2024.
Noorin said the panel found that the sentence imposed by the lower court was not commensurate with the seriousness of the offence against Jahidah.
"This is not an ordinary case of causing grievous hurt. The facts of the case demonstrate a brutal and continuous attack.
"The level of violence inflicted was so severe, resulting in the victim remaining in a permanent vegetative state to this day," she said.
The court heard that the medical evidence showed extensive and severe injuries, including brain haemorrhage, fractures in several parts of the body and serious neurological damage.
The judge said Jahidah's condition is irreversible and is fully dependent on others for the necessities of life.
Jahidah was described as a "living corpse' requiring full time care for years with no prospect of recovery and alive in name only, without the capacity to function meaningfully as a human being.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid sets RM1bil halal export target for Mara next year
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has instructed Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) to push sales of its halal products in the international market beyond RM1 billion next year.
Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister, said the target was necessary to move Bumiputera halal entrepreneurs beyond the domestic market and into the global halal supply chain.
He said Mara-linked halal products had recorded RM819 million in potential sales through international halal forum engagements, along with 13 memorandums of understanding worth more than RM70 million.
"This is a major achievement. It proves that Bumiputera products have a place and value in the global market.
"Next year is an important year. I want Mara to ensure sales of Mara halal products exceed RM1 billion in the international market," he said in his speech at the launch of Mara's halal ecosystem at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur.
Present were Deputy Rural and Regional Development Minister Datuk Rubiah Wang and Mara Chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki, Rural and Regional Development Ministry secretary-general Datuk Seri Suriani Ahmad and Mara director-general Datuk Zulfikri Osman.
Zahid said Mara had made encouraging progress in strengthening the halal agenda throughout the 12th Malaysia Plan.
He said 327 Mara entrepreneurs had obtained halal certification, while more than 1,000 entrepreneurs had been brought to the global stage through the Gate to Global programme.
"At the same time, I am targeting at least another 300 entrepreneurs to obtain halal certification this year.
"InsyaAllah, if we take the right steps, Mara's halal ecosystem will transform the position of Bumiputera entrepreneurs from local entrepreneurs into global exporters, and from the domestic market into the global halal value chain," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Factbox: What is the hantavirus that killed three cruise ship passengers?
Article:
PRAIA, Cape Verde: Three people have died and three are ill after a Netherlands-based cruise ship was hit by a
suspected outbreak of hantavirus
, authorities and media reports said on Sunday.
Here is information about the virus.
Spread by rodents
Hantavirus is primarily spread by rodents but can be transmitted between people in rare cases, according to the World Health Organization.
People most typically become infected when virus in rodent droppings, saliva and urine becomes airborne, such as when areas where rats and mice have nested are swept out.
According to The Lancet medical journal, the name comes from the Hantan River area of South Korea, where the virus was identified in the 1970s.
Can be fatal
Hantavirus is a family of viruses that cause two illnesses, one that primarily affects the lungs and the other that attacks the kidneys. The first gets the most attention because it has a high fatality rate of about 40 per cent.
Hantavirus pulmonary syndrome, the respiratory illness, is most commonly found in North and South America.
According to the Canadian government, about 200 cases of hantavirus pulmonary illness occur each year worldwide.
Concert pianist Betsy Arakawa, wife of actor Gene Hackman, died from hantavirus pulmonary syndrome in New Mexico in 2025, according to autopsy results.
Symptoms
Hantavirus usually begins with flu-like symptoms, such as fatigue and fever, one to eight weeks after exposure, according to the CDC.
Four to 10 days later, coughing, shortness of breath and fluid in the lungs appear.
Diagnosis in the first 72 hours of infection is difficult, the CDC says, so symptoms can easily be mistaken for flu.
Treatment
There is no specific therapy for hantavirus infection, so treatment focuses on supportive care including rest and fluids. Patients may need breathing support such as a ventilator.
Prevention
Experts say exposure to hantavirus can be minimised by deterring and eliminating rodents from areas where people are. Avoid vacuuming or sweeping dried droppings, which can aerosolise the virus.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sumud Nusantara Command Centre begins seven-day countdown for second Gaza flotilla
Article:
SEPANG: The Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) has officially initiated a seven-day countdown for the second wave of its "Break the Silence and Break the Siege" campaign, signalling that a fresh humanitarian flotilla mission to Gaza is imminent.
SNCC director Datuk Dr Sani Araby Alim Araby said the countdown serves as a "call to arms" for readiness rather than a fixed departure date.
"This is a countdown; we can announce the sailing at any given time. It is not strictly fixed at seven days," he told reporters at a SNCC press conference today.
He urged the Malaysian public and the international community to intensify advocacy efforts and maintain pressure on the global stage as the mission prepares for its next phase.
The announcement follows the safe arrival of 10 Malaysians in Turkiye on May 1, after being evacuated from Greece following their abduction during the original Sumud Flotilla mission last month.
Sani confirmed that all 10 activists have undergone medical examinations and are in stable condition, adding that instead of returning to Malaysia, they have been placed on high alert for redeployment.
"The struggle continues. These are experienced individuals who have participated in similar missions before. We will be deploying them when the time comes," he said.
The SNCC is currently working to consolidate its fleet, drawing on vessels stationed in both Greece and Turkiye to form a unified front for the second sailing.
The mission faces significant logistical hurdles following the illegal seizure of 21 ships by the Israel Defence Forces (IDF) on April 30, with only three vessels successfully recovered and towed by the Open Arms vessel from Barcelona.
He also confirmed that the Goleta has been sunk, with deliberate sabotage suspected.
"The Goleta is gone, and we suspect deliberate sabotage. It is not easy to tow vessels that have been intentionally damaged by the regime," he said, adding that 17 other vessels remain adrift with recovery efforts underway.
As the countdown progresses, the SNCC has issued a nationwide call for Malaysians to support the mission through digital advocacy, urging the public to counter narratives on social media while calling on the Muslim community to perform solat hajat and qunut nazilah in solidarity.
The SNCC also expressed gratitude for support from the government and the international community.
"The Speaker of the Dewan Rakyat, members of Parliament from across the divide, government ministries, and NGOs have all stood with us. Internationally, we have seen condemnation of Israel's actions from the United Nations, the OIC, and the leaders of Malaysia, Spain, and Turkiye."
"The regime is now in an increasingly desperate position. The world is rising, and that is exactly what 'Break the Silence' is meant to achieve," Sani added.
Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari was also present at the SNCC press conference today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Health Ministry's development budget safe despite austerity drive
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Health Ministry's development budget will not be affected by the government's austerity drive, with funding for essential healthcare infrastructure and services set to be preserved.
Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said while the ministry remained open to trimming non-essential expenditure, development allocations and core services would continue to be prioritised.
"Development is a critical component in completing our facilities and strengthening service delivery. While there may be areas where cuts can be considered, essential and major components must be protected," he told reporters at a press conference here today.
He also expressed appreciation to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim for agreeing to review a proposed RM3.04 billion budget cut to the ministry.
"I am deeply grateful to the prime minister for responding to our request to reconsider the proposed reduction by the Treasury secretary-general. Matters involving essential services and human resources must be safeguarded," he added.
Dzulkefly said that the ministry had submitted proposals identifying areas where adjustments could be made, although its preferred outcome would be to avoid cuts altogether.
In line with efforts to ensure prudent spending, he said the ministry would optimise the use of space in healthcare facilities and move away from designs perceived as overly luxurious.
"There is a perception that some facilities are designed with excessive space. What we aim for is optimisation — ensuring that every element is functional and cost-effective," he said.
He said that this would involve more practical spatial planning for areas such as corridors and lobbies, guided by value engineering and assessment to maximise efficiency.
"These will be our key criteria — functionality and cost-effectiveness," he said, adding that future projects, including the new Klinik Kesihatan Menjalara, would adopt vertical, multi-storey designs.
Earlier today, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced a
review
of the "luxurious scope" of hospital projects as part of broader fiscal discipline measures.
Dzulkefly was speaking after a site visit to the proposed Klinik Kesihatan Menjalara, which has been approved under the 13th Malaysia Plan with an allocation of RM85.9 million.
The facility, expected to begin construction in 2028 and be completed by January 2032, will serve some 256,000 residents and help ease congestion at existing clinics, particularly in Jinjang.
It will offer integrated services, including outpatient care, maternal and child health, dental services, emergency treatment and diagnostics under one roof.
Segambut member of parliament Hannah Yeoh said the project was the result of years of effort.
"After eight years of raising this in Parliament and addressing land issues, the clinic has finally been approved. There is a clear need for a larger facility in the area," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tiffin carrier becomes symbol of appreciation for Pahang's 'red heroes'
Article:
KUANTAN: A familiar household item, the mangkuk tingkat, is taking on a special role this World Firefighters' Day, as the tradition of sending meals in tiered tiffin carriers is repurposed to express appreciation and support for firefighters on duty.
The initiative by the Fire and Rescue Department's Women Officers and Firefighters' Wives Association (Perisma) Pahang features a special edition tiffin carrier in recognition of firefighters' round-the-clock commitment to public safety.
Perisma Pahang chairman Datin Suraya Jusoh said the tradition-inspired effort has resonated with the public.
"When we create something unique, especially related to the fire service, people want to own it. It helps build support for our 'red heroes' while reflecting our appreciation," she told Bernama.
The tiffin carrier, also known as siya, incorporates design elements representing women firefighters, highlighting their role within the rescue force.
Suraya said the second-edition tiffin carrier, following an earlier release during Aidilfitri, has drawn strong interest since being promoted on social media last week, including orders from Brunei.
"Initial production was small, but demand grew quickly after it went viral, and we had to increase output," she said, adding that the item is priced at RM29.90 per unit.
Proceeds will go towards Perisma's welfare programmes, particularly for firefighters' families in Pahang.
She also expressed appreciation to fire personnel and officers who contributed to the initiative's success, noting that the strong public response reflects growing recognition of firefighters' sacrifices while strengthening ties between the force and the community.
World Firefighters' Day, observed annually on May 4, honours the sacrifices and service of firefighters in ensuring public safety.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perak govt extends counselling support to family of teen stabbed 61 times
Article:
PARIT BUNTAR: The state government has mobilised counselling support for the family of a teenage girl who was killed in a brutal stabbing incident, with 61 wounds found on her body.
State Women, Family, Social Welfare and Entrepreneur Development Committee chairman Datuk Salbiah Mohamed said the Social Welfare Department had deployed psychologists to provide trauma counselling to the victim's family in Kuala Kangsar.
She said the loss was sudden and deeply distressing, particularly given the circumstances surrounding the incident.
"It is not easy to lose a loved one in the blink of an eye, especially under such circumstances. We understand the emotional toll this has taken on the family, and we extend our deepest sympathies," she told reporters after distributing Perak Sejahtera Cards at Mydin Parit Buntar here today.
Salbiah said department officers had visited the family earlier to offer immediate psychological support and would continue to monitor their well-being.
She said that Lintang assemblyman Datuk Mohd Zolkafly Harun had also visited the family to offer his condolences shortly after the incident.
She said the case underscored the growing need to address emotional and mental health issues at the community level.
"We are seeing more cases like this, and under my portfolio, as well as programmes involving women in Perak, we are placing greater focus on emotional management and mental health awareness.
"We need to strengthen efforts to ensure individuals are better equipped to manage their emotions and seek help when needed," she added.
Salbiah said that it was difficult to comprehend how such an act could be committed by someone in a rational state of mind, stressing the importance of early intervention and support systems.
The state government, she said, would continue enhancing initiatives aimed at promoting mental well-being and preventing similar tragedies.
It was reported that 19-year-old college student Nurfisya Zulkifly was found dead by the roadside in Kampung Padang Simah, Ketereh, at about 12.30am last Friday, with 61 stab wounds.
She was identified through fingerprint records after being found without identification, and is believed to have been killed elsewhere before her body was dumped at the scene.
Police have detained four people, including two 19-year-old boys, to assist in the investigation.
An elderly couple, aged 60 and 66 and believed to be the parents of one of the suspects, have since been
released
on police bail.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: King extends International Firefighters' Day greetings
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today extended International Firefighters' Day 2026 greetings to personnel of the  Fire and Rescue Department.
In a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar's Facebook page, His Majesty expressed appreciation for the service, sacrifices, and steadfast commitment of the Red Heroes, who are always ready to risk their lives for public safety and national well-being.
Sultan Ibrahim also expressed pride in the vital role the department plays in handling emergencies, from fires and natural disasters to rescue operations.
The King also prayed for the safety and protection of all firefighters and personnel as they carry out their duties.
"Al-Fatihah for those who have left us forever. May they be placed among the faithful and the martyrs," Sultan Ibrahim said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Petai smuggling busted in Kelantan border raid
Article:
RANTAU PANJANG: The General Operations Force (GOF) detained a man and seized 30 packets of smuggled petai during an operation at Kampung Lubok Gong here yesterday.
GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the seizure was made by the Ninth Battalion's Strike Force during routine border patrols at 5.30pm.
"Personnel spotted a suspected Proton Wira at Kampung Lubok Gong and proceeded to stop the vehicle for inspection," he said.
"Checks revealed plastic packets containing petai without valid documentation. A 40-year-old local man was detained after failing to produce any relevant papers."
He added that initial inspections also found the petai to be suspected of being infested with pests and diseases.
"The produce is believed to have been smuggled from Thailand for local distribution," he said.
Ahmad Radzi said the total value of the seizure, including the vehicle and the consignment of petai, was estimated at RM12,400.
All seized items have been handed over to the Kelantan Agriculture Department for further action.
The case is being investigated under Section 6 of the Plant Quarantine Act 1976 (Act 167).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Media Prima contributes RM10,000 to Pekan girls' home
Article:
PEKAN: Media Prima Bhd (MPB) has contributed RM10,000 to the Darussalam Al Mukhtar Home for orphans and underprivileged girls here to support education for its beneficiaries.
MPB group chairman Datuk Seri Dr Syed Hussian Aljunid handed over the mock cheque to the home's caretaker, Nik Ibrahim Nik Abdullah, in a ceremony witnessed by the Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah.
Syed Hussian described the initiative as part of MPB's efforts to support the sustainability of welfare institutions and strengthen educational opportunities for underprivileged groups.
He said the contribution would be fully utilised to ease the Darussalam Al Mukhtar home's daily operational costs.
"The allocation will also be channelled towards providing educational materials and supporting co-curricular activities to ensure the holistic development of the children.
"Through this contribution, MPB reinforces its commitment to Corporate Social Responsibility, particularly in supporting welfare institutions," he said.
Syed Hussian was met after attending the recording of TV3's Bersamamu Aidiladha Special programme and the handing over of business tithes at the Darussalam Al Mukhtar Home here today.
Also present were Tengku Mahkota Pahang, Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah.
Others present were MPB group chief financial officer Rosli Sabarudin and New Straits Times Press Editorial Content Support General Manager Datuk Muzli Mohamad Zin.
Syed Hussian expressed appreciation to Al-Sultan Abdullah and Tengku Hassanal for being part of the programme here.
He also expressed his gratitude to sponsor Syarikat Faiza Sdn Bhd for its contribution and commitment through the Group's broadcasting platforms, particularly TV3's iconic documentary programme Bersamamu, which remains dedicated to highlighting the realities of life.
Opened in 2017, the Darussalam Al Mukhtar Home for Orphaned and Underprivileged Girls, located along Jalan Rompin Lama here, is a shelter dedicated to caring for orphans and underprivileged girls in the district.
The premises, which currently houses 13 female students aged between eight and 17, emphasise balanced character development through Islamic education modules, formal schooling, and co-curricular activities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Death toll from Turkiye school shooting rises to 10
Article:
ISTANBUL: An 11-year-old girl has died of injuries sustained in a school shooting in southern Turkiye more than two weeks ago, bringing the death toll to 10, media reported Monday.
Almina Agaoglu died in hospital, the private NTV television reported.
A 14-year-old opened fire at a school in Kahramanmaras province on April 15, killing nine students aged 10 and 11 and one teacher. The youth attacker died at the scene.
Authorities said the boy brought five firearms to the school and was the son of a former police inspector, who has since been arrested.
In a separate incident the previous day in the southeastern province of Sanliurfa, a former student opened fire at his former high school, wounding 16 people, before taking his own life when confronted by police.
The attacks have sparked public outrage. President Recep Tayyip Erdogan has dismissed a deputy education minister and said the government would introduce measures, including restrictions on gun ownership.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PNB's 118 Mall set for August debut
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A major new retail landmark is taking shape beneath the world's second-tallest tower, as 118 Mall prepares for its official opening this August in the heart of the Merdeka 118 precinct.
Owned and managed by PNB Merdeka Ventures Sdn Bhd, a subsidiary of Permodalan Nasional Bhd, the mall is positioned within one of Kuala Lumpur's most high-profile urban developments.
According to a statement, 118 Mall is now in its final phase of preparation, with early tenants already beginning interior fit-outs.
PNB Merdeka Ventures Sdn Bhd chief executive officer Datuk Ir Ts Izwan Hasli Ibrahim said the precinct is evolving into a global destination combining retail, hospitality and cultural attractions.
"118 Mall plays an important role in completing the precinct experience by connecting retail, hospitality, culture and community into one seamless destination," he said, adding that more than 70 per cent of retail space has already been committed.
118 Mall retail head Sue Wang said the tenant mix was carefully curated to reflect Kuala Lumpur's identity.
"We have been very intentional about creating a mix that feels connected to this specific part of Kuala Lumpur.
"118 Mall is the heartbeat of the Merdeka 118 precinct, it gives our retailers a meaningful stage to shine and ensures the mall feels vibrant and welcoming to all," she said.
With its official launch set for August, 118 Mall is positioning itself as a key addition to Kuala Lumpur's retail landscape, offering a blend of lifestyle, dining and cultural experiences under its brand promise: "Live Your Story."
The mall is located within walking distance of Petaling Street and near historic landmarks such as Stadium Merdeka and Stadium Negara, reinforcing its positioning as a destination that blends heritage with modern city living.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman's home vandalised, family threatened over unpaid debt by loan shark
Article:
JASIN: A female trader faced a terrifying ordeal when her rented house in Taman Scientex Jasin Mutiara was allegedly harassed by loan sharks on Saturday.
In the 2pm incident, the suspect allegedly threw a bottle of chilli sauce at the house and a brick at the windshield of the victim's Proton Persona.
According to a source, the 25-year-old victim became aware of the incident after being contacted by her landlord, who was informed by a neighbour about suspicious activity in front of the house.
It is understood that the victim had previously dealt with an individual offering loans through WhatsApp.
She reportedly agreed to a loan of RM10,000 in mid-April before receiving RM6,500 and making repayments in stages.
She later took a second loan of RM20,000 and received RM15,000 in stages, requiring her to follow a set payment schedule.
The suspect began threatening the woman after she allegedly failed to make repayments, including sending pictures of her family's house in Pontian, Johor, and threatening to reveal her personal information.
On the day of the incident, the suspect sent a message claiming the victim had received a gift, believed to refer to the act of vandalism.
The victim also found three written notices demanding payment, which were believed to have been left by the suspect at the scene.
Fearing for her safety and that of her family, the victim lodged a police report yesterday.
District Police Chief Superintendent Lee Robert confirmed the incident and said the case is being investigated under Section 5(2) of the Moneylenders Act 1951 for harassing a borrower.
Upon conviction, the offence carries a fine of between RM250,000 and RM1 million, or imprisonment for a term not exceeding five years, or both.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fisherman finds 'whale vomit', says burnt sample gave off fragrant smoke
Article:
KEMAMAN: A fisherman has come across what he believes could be a rare lump of whale vomit after discovering a wax-like object weighing about 20kg while squid jigging at sea.
Mohd Raffi Yusoff, 58, said he found the floating object about four nautical miles off Kuala Kemasik around 6pm last Thursday.
"I saw a white object bobbing in and out of the water. At first, I thought it was a buoy," he said when met at his home in Kampung Gong Chengal, Kemasik.
"I moved closer and realised it was a heavy lump, almost wax-like in texture."
He said he used a scoop net to retrieve the object, managing to cut off a portion weighing about 5kg, while the rest drifted away.
"It was very heavy. When I touched it, it felt soft and gave off a pleasant smell. I was alone at sea, so I only managed to take a portion of it as I was also a bit worried," he said.
Raffi said the object continued to emit a fragrant scent throughout the night while he continued squid jigging.
"After returning home about 11pm, I showed it to my wife, Zuliza Harun, 39, who then searched online. From what we found, it could possibly be whale vomit," he said.
He added that he even burned a small piece of it, which produced light smoke and a lingering fragrance.
Raffi said he had agreed to send the specimen to Universiti Malaysia Terengganu for further testing to verify its authenticity.
"If it is confirmed to be whale vomit, I am very grateful. I would also be open to selling it if there are interested buyers," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia can withstand external shocks: Treasury deputy sec-gen
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia has the capacity to withstand external economic shocks amid a more uncertain global landscape, supported by sound fundamentals and ongoing reforms, the Finance Ministry said.
Treasury deputy secretary-general (investment) Datuk Dr Shahrazat Ahmad said the global outlook is increasingly clouded by geopolitical tensions, supply chain shifts and rapid technological change, which are altering traditional patterns of trade and investment.
Representing Treasury secretary-general Datuk Johan Mahmood Merican, she said Malaysia's openness to global markets exposes it to these shifts, but also positions the country to tap emerging opportunities.
She noted that the country's resilience is supported by its diversified economic base across manufacturing, services and commodities, as well as a well-regulated financial system and prudent fiscal management.
At the same time, Malaysia is pursuing a forward-looking agenda focused on accelerating digitalisation, advancing the green transition and implementing structural reforms to enhance competitiveness and productivity.
Shahrazat said these efforts aim not only to sustain growth but also to ensure it is inclusive and aligned with long-term sustainability.
She was speaking at the inaugural Future Focus Series forum on Malaysia's strategic position in a turbulent global economy, organised by the Public Service Department through the Razak School of Government (RSOG) under the National Institute of Public Administration.
The forum, which drew more than 150 participants, served as a platform to strengthen public-private dialogue and develop strategies to address global economic uncertainties.
Discussions centred on key risks such as global trade realignments, technological disruptions, supply chain pressures and geopolitical conflicts, and their implications for Malaysia.
In her keynote address, Shahrazat also highlighted RSOG's role in strengthening leadership, governance and public policy innovation, which she said are critical to ensuring the public sector remains agile and resilient while supporting sustainable national development.
She said the dialogue reflects Malaysia's readiness to navigate uncertainty with foresight, seize opportunities with pragmatism and contribute to a more balanced and resilient global economy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah tourism resilient: New global hotel chains to create hundreds of jobs
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah government is ramping up efforts to bolster the state's tourism capacity, ensuring the industry remains resilient despite the ongoing Middle East crisis.
Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said key initiatives include establishing partnerships with global airlines to enhance connectivity, developing premium tourism products, and elevating service standards to international benchmarks.
"Through these efforts, we will position Sabah as both a premier holiday destination and a dynamic business hub," he said at the official opening of the Sheraton Kota Kinabalu.
"The sector remains resilient, backed by strong investor confidence."
Hajiji noted that several other major hotels are in various stages of completion, including Avani and Fairfield by Marriott, Grandis at The Peninsula, Club Med in Kuala Penyu, InterContinental in Papar, and Wyndham in Semporna.
These developments are set to add 2,740 rooms to the state's inventory—a testament, Hajiji said, to the industry's optimism during uncertain times.
"We are looking at hundreds of jobs, not just within the hotels but across supply chains and related services. Local suppliers, restaurants, and attractions will benefit tremendously," he said, adding that the new developments will attract high-value conferences and corporate travellers.
Sabah is targeting four million tourist arrivals this year.
As of February, the state has recorded 656,821 visitors.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor ramps up water security measures as drought risk intensifies in Sedili, Johor Lama
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: The Johor government has stepped up long-term water security initiatives for Sedili and Johor Lama as prolonged dry weather and the rising risk of meteorological drought threaten raw water stability across the state.
State Works, Transportation, Infrastructure and Communications Committee chairman Fazli Salleh said Johor has been identified as a high-risk state for drought conditions based on meteorological projections from December last year.
He warned that rainfall deficits could reach as high as 60 per cent below average in certain areas, particularly in July and August, raising serious concerns over raw water availability.
"Certain parts of Johor are expected to experience significant rainfall deficits of up to 60 per cent below normal levels, especially in July and August. This poses a direct risk to the raw water supply," he told the Johor State Legislative Assembly sitting here today.
Fazli said the situation at Sungai Ulu Sedili Kecil, which supplies the
Lok Heng Water Treatment Plant
, has reached a critical level due to the prolonged dry spell, affecting more than 4,000 consumers in Kota Tinggi.
He said immediate mitigation efforts are already in place, including water tanker deliveries, static water tanks, bottled water distribution and cloud seeding operations.
"To address the current situation, we have activated emergency response measures including alternative water supply via tankers, installation of static tanks, distribution of mineral water and cloud seeding activities," he said.
Fazli said structural upgrades are also being carried out by water operators and relevant agencies, including capacity expansion works at the Lok Heng treatment plant, development of groundwater wells, inter-river transfer systems and the commissioning of a desalination facility.
He said the
desalination plant
, relocated from Forest City to Lok Heng, is now operational and will serve as a permanent backup source during drought periods by treating saline raw water.
For the longer term, he said Johor is planning a major raw water transfer project from Sungai Lebam to Lok Heng, involving pipeline construction and a pumping station in Bandar Penawar, with an estimated cost of RM40 million, subject to approval.
Fazli said the broader strategy is aimed at reducing reliance on a single river source and strengthening Johor's resilience against climate variability.
He added that the current combined demand for Lok Heng and Waha stands at about six million litres per day under normal conditions, rising to 7.6 million litres during peak usage, underscoring the urgency of ongoing investments to secure a sustainable supply.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: NGO head denies defaming Mais in 3R-linked court case
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The president of a non-governmental organisation pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court to allegedly defaming the Selangor Islamic Religious Council (Mais).
Mohd Ridzuan Abdullah, 58, is accused of making defamatory statements linked to purported arrears payments for mosque construction, allegedly to harm Mais' reputation, as well as misusing the name of the palace.
He is alleged to have committed the offence at 1pm at the Bangunan Perdana, Prime Minister's Department, on April 13.
The charge framed under Section 500 of the Penal Code carries a maximum two-year jail term or a fine or both upon conviction.
Ridzuan, a father of five, entered his plea before magistrate Ezrene Zakariah.
Deputy public prosecutor Datuk Ahmad Sazali Omar, who appeared with Nor Azizah Aling, asked the court to set a RM10,000 with one surety bail.
"As this charge involves defamation against a state religious institution and touches on 3R issues (race, religion and royalty), I ask that a higher bail be imposed on the accused to ensure his attendance in court," he said.
However, lawyer T. Ravindran argued for a lower bail, saying that his client sells health products for a living, does not have a steady income and is the sole breadwinner for his family.
He said Ridzuan had contested the Kuala Langat parliamentary seat in the 15th General Election, which shows that he would comply with all court conditions.
Ezrene granted bail at RM5,000 with one surety and set June 22 for mention.
Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar confirmed receiving a report on the alleged defamatory statements against Mais.
Mais had said it would take legal action against parties spreading untrue allegations regarding purported arrears in payment for the construction of the Al-Madaniah mosque at Jalan USJ 18/7 in Subang Jaya.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court sets Dec 1 for Inspector Sheila obstruction of duty trial
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The magistrate's court here today set two days beginning Dec 1 for the trial of suspended police officer Inspector Sheila, who is charged with obstructing a policeman.
Magistrate Aina Azahra Arifin fixed the dates after deputy public prosecutor Hench Goh informed the court that a representation submitted by Sheila, whose real name is Sheila Sharon Steven Kumar, 38, had been rejected by the Attorney-General's Chambers.
"A full trial is set for two days on Dec 1 and 2," she said.
Sheila is accused of intentionally obstructing Lance Corporal Malvinderjit Singh Teerath Singh, 37, from carrying out his crime prevention patrol duties in front of a restaurant along Jalan Sultan Azlan Shah, Dang Wangi, here between 8.15pm and 8.30pm on Nov 4 last year.
The charge is framed under Section 186 of the Penal Code, which carries a maximum jail term of two years, a fine of up to RM10,000, or both, upon conviction.
At today's proceedings, Sheila was represented by lawyer M. Manoharan.
In the Nov 4 incident, Sheila allegedly accused the officer on duty of not knowing the law and belittled police actions, besides claiming that the officer had threatened a Pakistani motorcyclist.
Sheila has been suspended from duty since June 23, 2023, following several cases, including allegedly insulting a police officer and threatening an elderly woman.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Island operators hit by soaring fuel costs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Island operators across Malaysia are facing mounting operational pressures due to fuel costs rising by 50-70 per cent, placing severe strain on off-grid tourism businesses.
Perhentian Island Tourism Operators Association (5P) said rising fuel prices, combined with continued reliance on off-grid power generation is hurting island based operators.
This includes resort and chalet owners, tour providers, food and beverage businesses, and other small tourism-dependent enterprises.
5P chairperson Rafizah Munir said operating costs on the islands have risen significantl in recent months, driven largely by escalating fuel prices.
"Fuel is not just another expense for us, it is essential to sustain the livelihood of the people on the island. We rely on generators daily to keep basic operations running.
"The recent increases are becoming difficult to sustain, and we are reaching a point where businesses may have no choice but to pass on costs to consumers or scale back operations," she said.
Rafizah said global energy market volatility continues to place upward pressure on operating costs nationwide, with off-grid destinations such as the Perhentian Islands bearing a disproportionate burden.
With no access to national electricity grid, businesses on the islands depend heavily on diesel- and petrol-powered generators to sustain daily operations, ranging from accommodation facilities to food and beverage outlets.
She cautioned that prolonged cost escalation could gradually erode the competitiveness of the Perhentian Islands as a key tourism destination, with wider implications for the local tourism ecosystem that support hundreds of livelihoods.
This includes hospitality workers, boat operators and small business owners whose incomes are closely tied to visitor flows.
5P urged Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry to extend access to the subsidised diesel control system and subsidised petrol control system to island-based operators, in order to help mitigate rising fuel burdens.
The appeal comes as Malaysia's tourism sector continues its strong post-pandemic recovery, supported by momentum from Visit Malaysia 2026 (VM2026), which has driven steady growth in both domestic and international arrivals.
According to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM), more than 200 million domestic tourist trips were recorded in 2025, highlighting the sector's expanding role in supporting national economic activity.
5P said given the structural limitations of island infrastructure, a uniform subsidy framework may not adequately reflect the realities faced by off-grid destinations.
Instead, the association is advocating for a more targeted and adaptive policy mechanism that recognises the unique operational constraints of island-based tourism players.
It has proposed that the ministry consider introducing a special provision under existing subsidy schemes or developing a tailored mechanism that ensures transparency, accountability and efficient distribution.
Rafizah said with VM2026 approaching, the issue should be given urgent attention to safeguard the sustainability of island tourism and the communities that depend on it.
Kampung Mukut village chief Afli Abdul Karim said recently that soaring fuel costs are pushing up prices and weighing on tourism in Pulau Tioman's villages.
He said higher costs also affected tourism, with visitor numbers dropping sharply.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chegubard apologises to Nik Nazmi, admits his allegations were false, baseless
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A defamation suit filed by former Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad against activist cum blogger Badrul Hisham Shaharin has been resolved after the latter issued a public apology at the High Court here today.
Badrul Hisham, better known as Chegubard, read out the consent judgment, acknowledging that his allegations against Nik Nazmi were false and baseless.
"I withdraw all allegations against Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad. I sincerely apologise to him for the embarrassment, hardship and distress caused.
"I undertake not to repeat or publish any further statements or allegations on the same matter or of a similar nature," he said.
The blogger also agreed to remove all disputed statements and related links from his social media accounts and relevant websites.
Judge Gan Techiong then recorded the consent judgment in accordance with the agreed terms of settlement.
"Under this order, the defendant must publish a video apology on any of his social media accounts within 24 hours. Please comply to avoid contempt of court," the judge said.
Earlier, counsel Razlan Hadri Zulkifli, representing Nik Nazmi, informed the court that both parties had reached a settlement and agreed to the consent judgment, which was confirmed by defence counsel Zolazrai Zolkapli.
Speaking to reporters after the proceedings, Nik Nazmi said he did not wish to prolong the matter and accepted the apology.
Nik Nazmi filed the suit on Jan 15, 2024, alleging that Badrul Hisham had posted five defamatory statements on his Facebook account, "Che GuBard", on Dec 20 and 29, 2023, concerning a flood mitigation project.
The plaintiff claimed the statements suggested he had lied about an open tender process and alleged corruption in the project, which was said to have driven costs up to RM16.6 billion.
"The defendant acted maliciously to damage my credibility and image as a politician, leading to public criticism and suspicion among my constituents. My personal and professional reputation has been affected," he said in his statement of claim.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Freak storm batters Pontian, fells trees in JB as volatile weather grips Johor
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A freak afternoon storm swept through Kampung Melayu Raya in Pekan Nenas, Pontian yesterday, ripping off roofs and forcing families to flee as violent winds and heavy rain struck with little warning.
No fatalities were reported.
The 4pm
squall
damaged 28 houses and displaced 100 people from 24 families, with residents abandoning their homes as debris was hurled across the village and rainwater poured in through shattered roofs.
Currently, 77 victims from 23 families are taking shelter at the SK Melayu Raya temporary relief centre, which was activated yesterday. Six families comprising 18 people have chosen to remain in their damaged homes.
Several residents described moments of panic as the storm intensified within minutes. One villager said his family
took cover
under a dining table as their house shook violently, while sections of the roof were torn off and flung more than 50m away.
State Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Muhammad Tarmizi Md Jaafar said the department responded to one storm-related incident and three cases of fallen trees following the squall.
"We attended to three cases of fallen trees in Ayer Hitam, Yong Peng and Pekan Nenas within hours of the storm. In each case, the trees either blocked roads or posed risks to nearby structures," he said.
"Our teams used chainsaws to clear the debris and restore access. All operations were completed swiftly and, fortunately, no casualties were reported."
The Malaysian Meteorological Department had earlier issued a warning of heavy rain, strong winds and thunderstorms across Johor, including Pontian, Kluang and Johor Baru.
The New Straits Times learnt that such storms were typical during the inter-monsoon period, when rapidly developing localised thunderstorms can unleash powerful wind gusts within minutes due to intense heat, moisture build-up and atmospheric instability.
In a separate incident in Kluang yesterday, a 32-year-old hiker collapsed while ascending Gunung Lambak and was later pronounced dead after a difficult evacuation hampered by heavy rain.
Tarmizi urged the public to exercise caution, warning that sudden storms and strong winds could strike with little notice during the current weather phase.
He advised against outdoor activities during unstable conditions, particularly in the late afternoon when convective storms are most likely to develop.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Factory worker dies after collapsing on Gunung Lambak
Article:
KLUANG: A factory worker died after losing consciousness at a height of 800 metres from the base of Gunung Lambak here yesterday.
Kluang district police chief ACP Bahrin Mohd Noh said the victim, Muhamad Nur Hakimi Mohd Haris, 32, from Felda Sungai Sibol, Kulai was descending the hill with three of his friends when he lost consciousness.
He added that the police received a distress call at 1.42pm and a team rushed to the scene, along with personnel from the Fire and Rescue Department and the Civil Defence Force.
"Heavy rain complicated the operation and Muhamad Nur Hakimi was brought down at 6.15pm, and was pronounced dead by a medical assistant officer," he said in a statement today.
The body was taken to the Enche' Besar Hajjah Khalsom Hospital forensics unit for further action, he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tears roll down Jahidah's cheeks when informed of her ex-husband's jail term — Sister
Article:
SEREMBAN: A woman who has been bedridden and in a vegetative state for five years following a domestic violence incident was moved to tears after learning that her former husband's prison sentence had been increased to 14 years by the Court of Appeal.
Jahidah Nordin, 48, reportedly reacted emotionally after her sister, Fauziah Nordin, 45, informed her of the court's decision involving her ex-husband, Rosmaini Abdul Raof.
Fauziah said she was deeply emotional upon reading the ruling and immediately went to her sister to share the news.
"I went home and told my sister. Even though she did not respond, I told her about the sentence.
"I noticed a change in her expression — she looked calmer, relieved, and then tears rolled down her face. I also could not hold back my emotions," she said.
She added that while the heavier sentence cannot undo the suffering endured, she is relieved with the court's decision.
Fauziah said the additional sentence and compensation imposed on Rosmaini may be the result of Jahidah's prayers, as she was the most severely wronged.
"We previously prayed that the man would receive a heavier sentence. I am very grateful to the public for their continued support," she said.
Regarding the RM25,000 compensation to be paid by Rosmaini to Jahidah, Fauziah said the money would be used to raise Jahidah's son, who is now five years old.
"If he pays the money, we can use it to raise Nufail. That is, if he has the money; if not, he will face an additional six months in prison," she said.
"The man seemingly has not learned his lesson. To women out there, please be careful around such individuals," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Ibu… forgive me' — teen who attacked adoptive mother pleads for mercy
Article:
BATU PAHAT: An 18-year-old youth pleaded for forgiveness from his adoptive mother after being charged in the magistrate's court here today with assaulting her and damaging household property when she refused to give him money.
"Ibu… I beg your forgiveness. I want to bow at your feet," he said before magistrate Arun Noval Dass after pleading guilty to two charges under Section 323 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 326A, and Section 427 of the same code.
On the first charge, the school dropout was accused of causing injury to his 66-year-old adoptive mother by punching her shoulder and pushing her to the ground.
Section 323 provides for a maximum jail term of one year or a fine of up to RM2,000, or both, while Section 326A allows for a sentence of up to twice the maximum term upon conviction.
On the second charge, he was accused of committing mischief by damaging a door, sofa, wall mirror, dining chair and flower pots belonging to the victim, causing losses estimated at RM5,400.
The offences were committed at a house in Taman Sri Jaya here at 1.30am on April 23.
According to the facts of the case, the accused had asked his adoptive mother for money on the night of the incident. When she reprimanded him and advised him against borrowing further, he became dissatisfied and assaulted her.
He then proceeded to kick and throw items in the house, including flower pots, and hurled a phone charger at a wall mirror, shattering it.
The victim later sought treatment at Sultanah Nora Ismail Hospital, where medical personnel confirmed injuries to her shoulder and back.
The prosecution was conducted by Inspector Aizuddin Sulaiman, while the accused was unrepresented.
The court fixed July 1 for mention of the Social Welfare Department's report, submission of case facts and sentencing.
Bail was set at RM2,000, but the accused was unable to post bail and was remanded at the Muar Correctional Centre.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perikatan to contest all 56 Johor seats, open to Muda collaboration in upcoming state polls
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 4 — The Opposition Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition intends to contest all 56 state assembly seats in the upcoming Johor state election (PRN).
Johor Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) chief Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal said PN and its parties are prepared to face the state election at any time.
“PN will contest all the state constituency seats in Johor.
“The candidates’ names list will be submitted directly to the coalition’s central leadership for final endorsement,” he told reporters at a media conference during the state legislative assembly sitting in Bangunan Sultan Ismail in Kota Iskandar here today.
Also present was the Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (Muda) president Amira Aisya Abd Aziz.
Dr Sahruddin, who is also the Bukit Kepong assemblyman, said that PN are also not ruling out the possibility of a political collaboration with Muda for the upcoming state polls.
However, the former menteri besar said that the current focus is on the appointment of the Johor PN chairman’s position, which is still vacant.
“Bersatu party president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin has sent a letter with a proposed name on behalf of the party to PN’s top leadership.
“The letter has been sent for more than a month, pending a reply. At the moment, we are still waiting as no one has been named yet,” he said.
On Muda’s collaboration with PN in the state polls, Amira Aisya said the matter had not been finalised yet.
The Puteri Wangsa assemblyman said that the party had already initiated informal discussions with Johor PN.
“Muda will make an announcement on our decision when the time comes,” she said.
Amira Aisya also revealed that Muda plans to contest between five and 10 state seats in the upcoming Johor state election.
She said the party’s targets are mixed constituencies, involving both urban and rural areas.
On the hotly contested Puteri Wangsa constituency, Amira Aisya named herself as the incumbent candidate and added that Muda will defend the seat in the state election.
“We also welcome any party that is keen to contest in Puteri Wangsa,” she said.
Both Johor Pakatan Harapan (PH) parties, PKR and Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) are also eyeing the Puteri Wangsa seat for the coming polls.

Title: Pahang MB to meet Taiping Zoo over fate of Malaysian elephants at Japan’s Tennoji Zoo
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 4 — Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail said he will hold a meeting with the Taiping Zoo and Night Safari in the near future to discuss issues related to three elephants, Dara, Amoi and Kelat (DAK), currently at Tennoji Zoo, Japan.
He said the meeting aimed to obtain further clarification and examine the best steps to bring the mammals back following concerns raised by various parties regarding the welfare and survival of jumbos in that country which has four seasons.
“I, along with the Taiping Zoo authorities, will hold a discussion and meeting in the near future, even earlier than the meeting planned by the Perak state government.
“We are worried that something will happen to the elephants... we are working on bringing them back to Malaysia and we hope the people will support this effort because the voice of the people can influence a decision,” he told Bernama.
He said this after attending the recording of the
Ruang Bicara
programme, produced by Bernama TV at Wisma Bernama today.
Previously, Wan Rosdy urged that the three elephants be immediately brought back to their homeland and placed at the Kuala Gandah National Elephant Conservation Centre in Temerloh, which is more suitable for the habitat and natural needs of elephants.
The call follows the statement by the Natural Resources and Environment Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup, who emphasised that Dara, Amoi, and Kelat should be brought back to Malaysia from Japan if the implementation of the existing agreement is found to no longer align with conservation principles, wildlife welfare and current sensitivities.
Last Friday, Perak Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Sandrea Ng Shy Ching said that a meeting with stakeholders will be held this week to thoroughly discuss the issue of relocating the three elephants. — Bernama

Title: Backbenchers Club urge mitigation plan over proposed RM3b health budget restructuring
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 4 — The Madani Government Backbenchers Club (BBC) has urged the government to present a comprehensive mitigation plan following public concern over proposed projections for the restructuring of the national budget, particularly involving the health sector, which is estimated to reach RM3 billion.
Its chairman, Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa, said that while the need to strengthen the country’s fiscal position is understood in the current economic context, a clear and comprehensive mitigation plan must be presented to ensure that adjustments to allocations do not affect the quality of public healthcare services.
She said that to ensure the sustainability of the national healthcare system in the medium and long term, the implementation of initiatives outlined in the Health White Paper must be expedited, including a shift towards a more comprehensive, wellbeing-based healthcare approach.
“In line with this aspiration, the introduction of a basic Medical and Health Insurance and Takaful Plan (MHIT) should be expedited as an alternative financing mechanism to reduce reliance on federal funds, while ensuring an inclusive, sustainable and affordable healthcare system,” she said in a statement today.
Dr Zaliha, who is also the Sekijang Member of Parliament, said the BBC also proposed that the government temporarily defer the construction of new hospitals and prioritise upgrading existing facilities, including ensuring the availability of medical equipment.
She said efforts to strengthen local production capacity for medicines and medical devices must also be enhanced to ensure the country’s health supply security.
In addition, the agenda to refurbish dilapidated health clinics must remain a priority, with primary care facilities upgraded to at least the capacity of Type 4 Health Clinics, thereby enhancing treatment capabilities at the community level and reducing congestion at public hospitals.
She added that the Madani Medical Scheme should be continued, as it has proven to act as a buffer in reducing patients’ reliance on public hospitals for minor treatments.
Dr Zaliha said the welfare and sustainability of healthcare workers must continue to be given attention, with budget restructuring carried out carefully to avoid knock-on effects on workload, burnout and the migration of specialists to the private sector.
“As a strategic partner of the government, the BBC will continue to play a constructive system of checks and balances to ensure that every national financial policy is anchored on the well-being of the people and the sustainability of the public service system,” she said.
Earlier, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government’s expenditure restructuring measures would not adversely affect critical sectors such as health and education.
He said the recruitment of new doctors and nurses as well as the construction of clinics would not be affected, while school construction projects, including the addition of new classrooms, would proceed as planned. — Bernama

Title: Kedah KPDN foils illegal diesel transfer at Chinese cemetery
Article:
JITRA, May 4 — The Kedah branch of the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) has foiled an attempt to illegally transfer diesel at an open area of a Chinese cemetery in Sungai Pasir, Sungai Petani, yesterday.
Kedah KPDN Enforcement Chief Zalina Aziz said during the 2pm raid, authorities discovered diesel being transferred from a cargo lorry into a tanker lorry.
“The raid was the result of two weeks of intelligence by KPDN officers. Two men in their 40s and 50s, who were transferring diesel from a cargo lorry fitted with an intermediate bulk container (IBC) tank at the rear into a tanker lorry, were arrested.
“KPDN also seized approximately 2,900 litres of diesel worth RM44,848. In addition, one cargo lorry, one tanker lorry, two IBC tanks and equipment used for the transfer were confiscated, bringing the total value of the seizure to RM165,248,” she told reporters here today.
She added that further checks found both men did not possess any permit or authorisation to store the controlled goods.
“Firm action will be taken against any individual attempting to misuse controlled goods, and the public is encouraged to channel information or complaints via WhatsApp at 019-8488000, the hotline 1-800-886-800 or the complaint portal at e-aduan@kpdn.gov.my,” she said. — Bernama

Title: Putrajaya dengue cases drop 36.2pc, Wolbachia method shows success
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, May 4 — Dengue cases in Putrajaya fell by 36.2 per cent from January to April 2026 compared with the same period last year, with the implementation of Wolbachia technology identified as a key factor in enabling faster and more effective control of the urban outbreak.
Putrajaya Corporation (PPj) president Datuk Mohd Sakeri Abdul Kadir said the decrease shows that conventional approaches alone are no longer sufficient to address dengue challenges in densely populated urban areas.
“Data in Putrajaya shows that innovations such as the Wolbachia technology can accelerate dengue control and protect communities more effectively.
“At the same time, this effort is part of an ongoing commitment to make Putrajaya a safer and more conducive city, especially to ensure children can play in residential areas without worrying about health risks,” he said in a statement today.
Sharing the latest data, Mohd Sakeri said as of April 2026, the implementation of Wolbachia technology in Putrajaya has been carried out in stages in high-risk localities, namely Precinct 14, where 588,800 adult mosquitoes have been released in several phases since 2020.
The programme, said Mohd Sakeri, is now in the maintenance phase, including additional releases.
“In Precinct 18, a total of 554,400 adult mosquitoes have been released since October 2025, with the area now in the ‘sustain phase’, indicating that the Wolbachia mosquito population is stabilising,” he said.
He added that the Federal Territories Department is evaluating the effectiveness of existing budget allocations, including community clean-up programmes, while considering expanding Wolbachia technology to People’s Housing Projects (PPR) and public housing areas, which recorded high dengue case rates. — Bernama

Title: Deputy finance minister: Malaysia must become tech-driven nation to tackle low wages
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI, March 4 — Malaysia needs to shift from being perceived mainly as a trading nation to a technology-driven country by striving to produce its own technological innovations in order to tackle the issue of low wages in the country.
Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said the issue can be tackled if companies in the country, including in Johor, have more technological innovations, thereby creating jobs that offer higher salaries to locals.
According to him, the general perception that Malaysian companies merely support the needs of multinational corporations must also be changed with a new mindset within the government.
“Both the state and federal governments need to share responsibility in developing Malaysian companies into multinational corporations,” he said during the Johor state legislative assembly sitting at the Sultan Ismail Building here today.
Liew (DAP-Perling) said the low wage issue must be addressed as it is among the reasons behind the migration of hundreds of thousands of Johoreans as well as other Malaysian citizens to Singapore to become foreign workers.
Liew, who is also the Iskandar Puteri Member of Parliament, said many residents living in Greater Johor Bahru have expressed dissatisfaction about the rising cost of living following the influx of Singaporeans and corporate clients into Johor, which has become increasingly prosperous of late.
Meanwhile, Liew acknowledged the state government’s efforts under the leadership of Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi in developing the state.
According to him, various progress has been achieved over the past three years through joint efforts with the Federal Government, including initiatives such as the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ), which has placed Johor in the regional spotlight.
He added that Johor Bahru should also be seen as the country’s second economic capital after Kuala Lumpur, especially in the context of its role in the JS-SEZ.
“This also gives confidence that with Greater Johor Bahru being one of the economic guardians, Malaysia can become a more successful nation,” he said.
Greater Johor Bahru includes Johor Bahru city, Iskandar Puteri, Pasir Gudang, and Kulai, which are set to become key destinations for economic growth involving development, industrial, tourism, infrastructure, and real estate projects. — Bernama

Title: Knife-wielding robber flees with RM1,200 cash after threatening convenience store worker in Sarawak
Article:
KUCHING, May 4 — Police are on the lookout for a man believed to have been involved in a knife-point robbery at a 24-hour convenience store in Kota Sentosa earlier today.
